The '''Nat'xikta''' were a small, wealthy [[Fefze]] [[swarm]] that lived on [[Brolsam]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Pro-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]], the Nat'xikta supported the world's government by [[Spy/Legends|spying]] for them, which resulted in the swarm gaining influence. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Imperial officials and sympathizers were [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] by the local population after [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], from Imperial rule. The Nat'xikta were the main proponents of an effort to free the incarcerated Imperials and return the planet to their authority.

==History==
The Nat'xikta were a [[Fefze]] [[swarm]] that resided on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brolsam]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. A wealthy swarm, they supported the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] rulers of the world by [[Spy/Legends|spying]] for them. As a result, they accrued both power and status. They continued this arrangement when the planet came under the authority of a self-proclaimed [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]],<ref name="o" /> who had withdrawn the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] from the Empire following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the breakdown of Imperial authority.<ref name="tdc">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref>

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], in the wake of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] of the sector [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], Brolsam's disgruntled [[Human/Legends|Human]] and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] populations—along with a sizeable number of Fefze who were not affiliated with the Nat'xikta—began [[Prison/Legends|imprisoning]] Imperial officials and sympathizers. Imperial forces responded by resorting to guerrilla warfare, placing the planet on the edge of civil war. The Nat'xikta, afraid of losing the influence they wielded on the planet, hired Rodian [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to free the incarcerated Imperials and return them to power. To achieve their goal, the Nat'xikta were prepared to sacrifice hundreds of Fefze.<ref name="o" />

{{RPGseed}}
The Nat'xikta's plans were foiled by the crew of the ''[[FarStar]]'', a New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] that was visiting the planet searching for information regarding the whereabouts of Moff Sarne, who had fled Kal'Shebbol during the New Republic's assault on the planet.<ref name="o" />
{{Endgame}}

==Organization==
The Nat'xikta were a small swarm, with hundreds of individual Fefze contributing to the whole. The swarm was wealthy and had attained power by supporting the Imperial regime on Brolsam.<ref name="o" /> Despite being comprised of hundreds of Fefze, the Nat'xikta operated as a group hive mind.<ref name="GG4">[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', Second Edition]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Nat'xikta are featured in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]]. Although not featured directly in any adventure, the swarm are included in a specific "adventure hook" detailed in the scenario ''[[Omens]]''. This article assumes that the ''FarStar'' did indeed travel to Brolsam and interact with the Nat'xikta as detailed in the published adventure hook.
